execution_sequence.description: stable ANN IDs: `video_id+host -> int64`
video-page similars diversity + larger pools
video metadata completeness + refresh mutable fields
video-page UX flow for similars
description + single-like removal
popular weighted-random + feed modes
about outbound analytics
timestamped lifecycle logs + request correlation + static-page visit logs
cache runtime safety + similarity precompute scope + shadow swap + zero-downtime deploy
docs + docstrings
workflow CLI + sync/materialize/confirm automation
feature checklist sync and issue approved gate
F7 Draft lifecycle model and validation gate implementation
F7 plan issue draft-file input and Draft-state write behavior
F7 canonical plan and status command routing with legacy cleanup
F7 protocol and workflow rewrite for Draft validation lifecycle
F9 strict confirm feature cleanup contract and implementation
F9 explicit child-issue cascade mode for confirm feature
F9 issue-scoped execution commit policy and GitHub visibility guidance
F10 explicit feature issue publication contract and runtime
F10 explicit child issue publication and feature-owned issue batches
F10 publish-oriented help, workflow docs, and migration cleanup
F11 milestone execution-order block structure and parser support
F11 planning and sync maintenance for milestone feature execution order
F11 confirm cleanup and docs for milestone execution-order lifecycle

functional_blocks.title: Block A: Similarity and recommendation core
functional_blocks.scope: ANN/similarity defaults, impacted recompute, upnext diversity, popular-layer sampling quality.
functional_blocks.outcome: ANN switches to stable `ann_id` mapping, upnext refresh stops repeating the same 8 cards, and popular feed uses weighted-random by similarity instead of flat random sampling.

functional_blocks.title: Block B: Video page data + UX behavior
functional_blocks.scope: metadata completeness, fast similar rendering, comments, profile/likes interactions.
functional_blocks.outcome: video page loads similars immediately (without waiting for remote metadata), renders progressive similars from one larger batch on scroll, shows tags/category with mutable-field refresh, supports read-only comments with pagination, has collapsible description, and allows removing one like instead of only full reset.

functional_blocks.title: Block C: Feed and discovery product features
functional_blocks.scope: feed modes, search UX/API, crawler seed mode from one instance/subscriptions.
functional_blocks.outcome: home page gets explicit feed mode switch (`recommendations/hot/recent/random/popular`), backend exposes video search API (`/api/search/videos` with paging/sort), and crawler can start from one `--seed-instance` and expand through federated subscriptions.

functional_blocks.title: Block D: Analytics and style infrastructure
functional_blocks.scope: outbound click analytics and optional styling-system consolidation.
functional_blocks.outcome: About-page outbound links send tracked events to a dedicated API endpoint and SQLite analytics table (with validation/rate-limit path), while repeated styling patterns can be migrated to a shared Tailwind-based system for new blocks.

functional_blocks.title: Block E: Documentation and maintenance
functional_blocks.scope: recommendations docs alignment and missing docstrings.
functional_blocks.outcome: `RECOMMENDATIONS_OVERVIEW` is aligned with actual runtime pipeline (candidate generation/mixing/filters), and touched modules/functions/classes have explicit docstrings so behavior is readable without code archaeology.

functional_blocks.title: Block F: Logging and observability
functional_blocks.scope: explicit timestamped request logs, request-id correlation across request lifecycle, and static-page visit visibility for About/static informational pages.
functional_blocks.outcome: every API request gets `request-start -> work logs -> request-end` with one shared `request_id` and explicit timestamp format, and nginx adds dedicated visit logs for static informational routes with request-correlation-compatible fields.

functional_blocks.title: Block G: Runtime reliability and operations
functional_blocks.scope: safe cache refresh/swap runtime behavior (random + similarity), scoped similarity precompute updates, and automated blue/green nginx cutover.
functional_blocks.outcome: random/similarity caches refresh via shadow files + atomic swap, updater similarity stage can rewrite scoped cache sources instead of full rebuilds, and deploy script performs blue/green switch on `7070/7071` with health-check gate and rollback.

functional_blocks.title: Block H: Workflow automation for planning/materialization execution
functional_blocks.scope: unified workflow CLI, feature lifecycle base commands, tracker sync+validation automation, and completion/materialization command contracts.
functional_blocks.outcome: the process flow (`create/plan/approve/sync/materialize/execute/confirm`) runs through `dev/workflow` with deterministic tracker updates, `task_count`-based ID allocation, canonical feature branch handling, and confirmation cascade with GitHub close.

functional_blocks.title: Block J: Materialize mode split and issue-level gates
functional_blocks.scope: explicit materialize modes, scoped issue materialization, create-only behavior, feature checklist sync, and issue-level approval gate enforcement.
functional_blocks.outcome: workflow materialize becomes explicitly mode-driven, supports one-issue targeting, avoids unwanted edits in create-only mode, keeps feature issue checklist in sync, and enforces issue-level approval before execution and issue materialization.

functional_blocks.title: Block M: Draft planning lifecycle and canonical status surface
functional_blocks.scope: planning lifecycle status model, validation commands, draft-file issue planning, canonical status routing, and protocol/workflow alignment.
functional_blocks.outcome: planning flows explicitly move through Pending, Draft, Planned, and Tasked states, issue and feature validation become explicit gates, and compact status commands expose lifecycle state without tracker-file parsing.

functional_blocks.title: Block N: Confirm closure semantics and execution commit policy
functional_blocks.scope: strict feature-confirm cleanup gates, explicit subtree cascade behavior, and issue-scoped execution commit policy guidance.
functional_blocks.outcome: confirm feature distinguishes strict closure from explicit subtree cascade, FEATURE_PLANS cleanup becomes deterministic at both issue and feature levels, and execute issue has one documented commit policy with accurate GitHub visibility expectations.

functional_blocks.title: Block O: Explicit publish commands for GitHub issue creation
functional_blocks.scope: feature issue publication, child issue publication, feature-owned issue-batch publication, and publish-oriented help and workflow documentation.
functional_blocks.outcome: GitHub issue creation uses explicit publish feature, publish issue, and publish issues commands with automatic milestone ownership and one clear separation between local registration and remote publication.

functional_blocks.title: Block P: Milestone-level feature execution order planning block
functional_blocks.scope: milestone-scoped feature execution-order structure, planning and sync maintenance, and confirm-time cleanup with lifecycle documentation.
functional_blocks.outcome: FEATURE_PLANS gains one planning-owned milestone execution-order block that is maintained during feature planning and cleaned deterministically during feature confirmation.

overlaps.description: both touch popular layer output behavior.
same logging contract and request context propagation.
both rely on cross-log correlation (`request_id`, timestamp conventions, runbook).
both touch About-page observability and can overlap in intent (analytics vs logging).
both touch nginx-facing request metadata (`X-Request-ID` propagation and operational config).
same random-cache runtime path (background refresh, atomic swap, locks).
same operational primitives (shadow file build, atomic swap, lock-scoped reconnect).
both touch updater similarity stage and cache rewrite behavior.
deploy orchestration and cache cutover both affect runtime availability windows.
deploy safety depends on fast readiness and warm startup behavior.
command parser contract and feature command handlers share one CLI argument model.
sync write path and validation/ID-allocation rules must use one tracker model.
Draft-aware lifecycle helpers must be shared between base status semantics and plan issue write behavior.
Issue and feature validation should reuse one Gate 0 plan-block validation path instead of duplicating checks.
Canonical plan and status commands share one CLI surface and should align on compact deterministic output behavior.
Protocol docs must describe the exact compact status contract implemented by the runtime.
Workflow docs must reflect the exact canonical plan issue routing after legacy feature plan-issue removal.
Feature-level cleanup implementation must follow the audited confirm sequencing and existing issue cleanup primitives.
Cascade runtime should reuse the strict feature cleanup helper instead of implementing separate feature-section deletion logic.
Workflow guidance and help text must reflect the exact explicit cascade flag and its subtree semantics.
Commit policy wording must stay aligned with the documented GitHub visibility limits for child issues and parent feature issues.
Feature and child-issue publication contracts must share one consistent model for create-only publication, repeat runs, and milestone ownership.
Single-issue and feature-owned batch publication should reuse one publication engine and not duplicate issue creation or skip logic.
Workflow docs must reflect the exact feature-owned issue-batch publication behavior implemented by the runtime.
Help output and migration guidance must move together from materialize wording to publish wording to avoid mixed command models.
Plan-flow integration depends on one shared parser and lint understanding of the new milestone execution-order block.
Any sync or delta integration must reuse the same execution-order metadata contract introduced at the structure level.
Confirm cleanup semantics must align with the exact planning-ownership rules that govern when features enter the execution-order block.
Workflow documentation must describe the same confirm cleanup behavior implemented for milestone execution-order entries.
